families.name: Canonical sequence and recurrence
families.question: What does the repository Fv convention produce, and how is each destination known from the prior two?

families.name: Ratios, convergence, and error
families.question: How do adjacent ratios approach phi and 1/phi without finite equality?

families.name: Exact identities and discrete structures
families.question: Which exact Fibonacci identities, combinatorial structures, and integer patterns are visible?

families.name: Geometry, spirals, and golden forms
families.question: What geometric constructions are exact, approximate, or visually related?

families.name: Empirical and reality-test applications
families.question: What evidence would distinguish a Fibonacci claim from alternatives?

families.name: Metadata, systems, and workflow
families.question: How can recurrence illuminate documents, dependency, storage, prioritization, and cadence?

families.name: Normalized time metaphor
families.question: What does the declared 1/phi, 1, phi time coordinate reveal without becoming a theorem?

levels.name: Seed / premise
levels.role: Minimal statement of the idea, convention, variables, and caveats.

levels.name: Data / source table
levels.role: Canonical, computed, measured, or metaphorical source rows.

levels.name: Micro figure
levels.role: One-page or one-panel introduction with near-horizon scope.

levels.name: Core reference
levels.role: Mid-horizon or canonical artifact suitable as the baseline document.

levels.name: Comparative / analytic
levels.role: Contrasts views, models, residuals, or transformations.

levels.name: Audit / protocol
levels.role: Explicit checks, failure modes, assumptions, and evidence boundaries.

levels.name: Synthesis / generative suite
levels.role: Full chapter, package, or template family that can spawn variants.

seed_artifacts.role: Machine-readable horizontal/vertical coordinate map and generation rules.
Human-readable canonical Fv -1 through Fv 28 table with convention notes.
Forward ratios with discrete markers and phi reference line.
Guide grouping sums, Cassini, matrices, gcd, modulo, and decompositions.
Rules for square, rectangle, arc, center, tangency, and scaling geometry figures.
Evidence-boundary standard for empirical Fibonacci claims.
Yesterday/Today/Tomorrow coordinate values and metaphor boundaries.

generation_rules: same_horizontal_positive_vertical: keep horizontal fixed and move upward through higher vertical levels
same_horizontal_negative_vertical: keep horizontal fixed and generate supporting lower-level premises, data, manifests, and micro figures
across_horizontals_same_level: keep vertical level fixed and generate analogues in other horizontal families
across_horizontals_three_up_three_down: generate H1..H7 by levels source_level-3 through source_level+3, clipped to -3..+3
twenty_more_like_this: rank by same horizontal adjacent levels, same level across horizontals, adjacent horizontals adjacent levels
format_pairing: major md references should have json manifests when reusable and xlsx workbooks when tabular
graph_pairing: major svg figures should link to md explanation, json data, and xlsx workbook when applicable
evidence_boundary: empirical, application, and time-metaphor outputs must state what they do not prove
